# Questions in Livestream 205

Live Stream #205: Dead Seminaries Society

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:43</a> | What should someone do if they were accidentally baptized in an invalid way but their church will not rebaptize them and another church is not available? | He said the church should provide some path forward if it is really declaring the earlier baptism invalid, and he also stressed that baptism is not what saves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=882" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:42</a> | How can the sun be over Gibeon in Joshua 10:12 if Gibeon is north of the tropics? | He read the passage as an omen-laden astronomical sign for the Amorites, not as a simple modern-scientific description. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1046" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:26</a> | Why was the sun-and-moon sign special enough to write down? | He said it was special because it would have been a bad omen for the Amorites, attacking their morale through their own astrological assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:55</a> | Are zombies real? | Reanimated corpses are not real, but he said Haitian-style zombification using toxins to create mindless living servants is a real phenomenon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1315" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:55</a> | How should Exodus 20:4-6 be understood if it seems to prohibit all images while God commands cherubim on the ark? | He said the commandment prohibits worshiped idols, not all artwork or decorative sacred images. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:16</a> | Were almond flowers and pomegranates used in temple iconography? | Yes. He said they, like cherubim, were lawful temple images because they were not objects of worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1698" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:18</a> | What is your opinion on images or icons of Christ? | He saw most icon use as appeal or invocation rather than worship, though he did not think icons were efficacious and warned against worshiping them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=1945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:25</a> | How should someone read Titus Kennedy's book *Excavating the Evidence for Jesus*, if at all? | He said Kennedy's books are shallow but have useful photos, and that the Jesus book is better than the Old Testament one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=2109" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:09</a> | How can you write historical fiction about periods when the church did horrible things without whitewashing the atrocities or pushing readers away from Christianity? | He said the key is to tell the truth about the wrongdoing while refusing to equate Christ with every misuse of Christ's name. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=2580" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:00</a> | Would you be interested in doing a future video on Gnostic gospels? | Not especially. He said he finds them dull and not very rich in actual gnostic thought. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3319" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:19</a> | How do you study the Bible, such as the epistles; do you use an exegetical or hermeneutical checklist? | He said he does not use a checklist but practices attentive reading, looking carefully at context, language, and connections while using commentaries when helpful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:27</a> | Are dinosaurs real? | Yes. He said dinosaurs were real, though they are now extinct. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3525" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:45</a> | Who are the Emim and Zamzummim in Deuteronomy 2? | He treated them as old regional giant-clan or people-group labels preserved in the text's historical memory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:18</a> | Does Deuteronomy 2:10 imply the Anakim were huge? | Not strongly. He said it is a scribal gloss and later language made groups like Rephaim and Anakim synonymous with giants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:04</a> | Are trees the only things hard to date in the Levant? | He said wood is especially hard because of regional carbon and tree-ring issues, while annual crops and fabrics are easier when preserved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=3960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:00</a> | How are Matthew 5:38-39 related? | He said Jesus contrasts lex talionis with nonretaliation, moving from legal limits on revenge toward loving neighbor and enemy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=4291" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:31</a> | Is Manasseh called a half-tribe because half lived across the Jordan or because of Joseph and Ephraim? | He said it is because Joseph's inheritance was divided into Ephraim and Manasseh, not because of the Jordan settlement pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=4369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:49</a> | When are you going back to Egypt? | He was unsure whether another tour would happen because the previous tour had low signups and last-minute cancellations, but said it might be a year or two if it happens. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=4457" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:17</a> | Were the tribes on the east side of the Jordan only to return when all the other tribes conquered the land, even though some never did? | He said he would need the exact text, but tentatively took it as the land the tribes were actually going to conquer, not every promised tract in an absolute sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=4620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:00</a> | How do you move away from fundamentalism without abandoning the faith? | He answered by separating the core of the faith from the habits and assumptions that define fundamentalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=5189" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:29</a> | How important is biblical inerrancy to the Bible being considered God's word, and is that a false dichotomy? | He said inerrancy is important and foundational, especially for sola scriptura, but not strictly necessary for calling the Bible God's word. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=5695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:55</a> | Which archaeology book would David gift, apart from his own? | He recommended James K. Hoffmeier's *The Archaeology of the Bible* as a beautiful and useful gift book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=5909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:29</a> | Were the sacrifices in Leviticus 1:6 similar to sacrifices in Egypt during that time? | He said not really, though both systems could feed priests; Egyptian offerings were usually presented and redistributed rather than handled like Levitical burnt offerings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:34</a> | Is it true that children made to pass through the fire were then eaten? | No. He said this referred to child sacrifice as a burnt offering, with no evidence of cannibalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6179" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:59</a> | Is passing children through the fire Moloch? | He said Moloch appears to refer to a kind of child-sacrifice offering, not to a known ancient Near Eastern god. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:42</a> | What is your favorite biblical archaeological find? | He chose the Merneptah Victory Stela because of its early reference to Israel, firm date, and useful political context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:57</a> | If Leviticus foreshadows the sacrifice of Jesus, how can we avoid missing its message to its original audience? | He recommended a Christotelic approach: start with the original audience and context, then move to Christ as the fulfillment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6565" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:25</a> | What year is most likely for Christ's crucifixion and resurrection? | He said AD 30 is probably more likely than AD 33, though those are the main possible years in the relevant Passover and Sabbath calculations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=6955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:55</a> | What is going on in Mark 14:51-52 with the young man who fled naked? | He said nobody knows for sure; it could be Mark inserting himself or a literary representation of the disciples fleeing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=7083" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:03</a> | What did the Jews do about dirty diapers? | He said they cleaned them, as everyone did before disposable diapers, and it was not a major ritual issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=7156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:16</a> | Why does Mary being full of grace in Luke 1:28 mean she was sinless, when Stephen is also called full of grace in some translations? | He said it does not mean sinless; it means Mary was highly favored, and reading sinlessness from the phrase is eisegesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=7845" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:45</a> | If manna is mushrooms, does that contradict John 6:31-35? | No. He said "from heaven" means God-delivered, and Exodus describes manna appearing after dew evaporates rather than visibly falling from the sky. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=8616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:36</a> | What is the difference between our soul and our spirit, or are they the same thing? | He said both bipartite and tripartite views have arguments, and he does not hold a firm settled answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=8771" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:11</a> | Did priests see a theophany, light, or fire between the cherubim, and how could Nadab and Abihu trespass if God was visibly there? | He said there was normally no visible fire or light between the cherubim, and Nadab and Abihu likely acted carelessly with strange fire rather than seeing a constant visible warning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:13</a> | Could Yahweh be derived from adding an H to the end because Hebrew did not like final vowels except for possessive pronouns? | He said that is possible, and that Yahweh, Yahoo, and Yah could relate through several shortening or spelling patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:47</a> | Were Midianites worshipers of Yahoo while later Hebrew made it the full tetragrammaton? | He said we do not know because we lack Midianite writings; the main evidence is the biblical material about Moses and his father-in-law. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9238" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:58</a> | What is Qos? | He described Qos as the great god of Edom, attested in limited seals or inscriptions, with uncertain etymology and an unclear relationship to Edomite monotheistic tendencies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:01</a> | How can Christians pursue justice in a corrupt country if personal vengeance is not allowed? | He said Christians should seek legal redress where possible, act through love and prayer, and leave vengeance or final justice to the Lord when earthly justice fails. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9589" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:49</a> | Why was Samson allowed and blessed to get personal vengeance? | He said Samson was a judge and a tool, not a moral model, and that the blessing was tied to the Nazarite vow rather than an endorsement of all his actions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=9878" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:38</a> | What do you make of the claim that Yam Suph is a judgment against Yam from the Baal Cycle? | He rejected the argument, saying it forces the Baal Cycle onto an Exodus itinerary made up of real, documented places. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=10169" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:29</a> | Can we make a geopolitical case for the Exodus in light of Egypt, the Hittites, and the Battle of Kadesh? | Yes. He connected a late Exodus and Israel's entry to Egypt's civil conflict, Hittite collapse, Philistine pressure, and late Bronze destruction patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=10381" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:01</a> | Will *Reliquary of the Dead* be a 2D adventure game or an FMV game? | He said there are no plans to make it a video game; an audiobook or television adaptation would be more plausible if the book sold well enough. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:21</a> | Who is the most likable character in the book? | He and the producer pointed to Mahat or Melissa, with joking mentions of the cobra and scatterbug. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:56</a> | What is the likely date range for the Gospel of John? | He said most scholars place it around 80-95 AD, while deferring to D. A. Carson for details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:02</a> | Is the "two powers in heaven" idea evident elsewhere in the Old Testament beyond Daniel 7? | Yes. He pointed to Genesis 1, Babel, the angel of the Lord, and Isaiah 44:6. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11345" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:05</a> | If Josephus and Manetho got the Exodus pharaoh wrong, what evidence do early-date advocates have for Amenhotep II? | He said Manetho traditions conflict, and without them the case mostly rests on a contested reading of 1 Kings 6:1. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11666" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:26</a> | Who was the Semitic chariot driver for Ramesses II? | He said we do not know; Kadesh material mentions a driver but not by name, and such a person would likely have been highly trusted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=11846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:26</a> | What makes a doctrine or belief actually heretical? | He said heresy must compromise an essential Christian doctrine, usually about God, humanity, salvation, or core dogma; secondary disagreements can be wrong without being heretical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=12401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:41</a> | What if you do not have the evidence to hand? | He said following the evidence means not speaking beyond it; possible future evidence should be framed as hope or uncertainty, not treated as proof. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=12924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:24</a> | What was the name of God known as at the Tower of Babel: Anu, El, or Jehovah? | He said we do not know, but *ilum* is a plausible generic guess if the setting involved Akkadian or a West Semitic dialect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=13061" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:41</a> | Is whispering in tombs common practice among Egyptologists and archaeologists, or only among people with tourist-group degrees? | He said whispering is not normally required unless other people are in a public tomb and you are trying not to disturb them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=13155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:15</a> | Is there proof or evidence that the port of Avaris was used to leave Egypt rather than going with Moses, and where would the people go? | He said no; the port route would head through a controlled choke point toward the Mediterranean, while the Exodus route fits movement south through Pi-Ramesses and Wadi Tumilat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=13417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:37</a> | Are 1 Samuel 16:14-23 and chapter 17 out of chronological order, or is there another way to read them? | He did not see a clear chronological disorder, though he allowed that details such as David's armor-bearer status could be retrojected. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=13765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:25</a> | Could you discuss obeying God versus doing good works? | He said good works do not save, but God prepares good works for believers to walk in, and those works perfect faith. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=14116" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:16</a> | Do we have a guardian angel or guardian angels? | He said Matthew 18 gives warrant for guardian angels, especially for children, but Scripture does not say whether guardians are personal or shared. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9X7hVNRfNUw?t=14385" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:45</a> | What is the history and tradition behind gargoyles on Roman Catholic and Anglican churches? | He said gargoyles are medieval mockery of devils or demons, meant to make prideful evil ridiculous. |
